clues.title: The Invitation
clues.description: A cream-coloured card on heavy stock, embossed with Lady Ashworth's crest. The ink is violet — an unusual choice. On the reverse, in a different hand, someone has written a sequence of numbers: 3-7-13.

clues.title: The Hidden Mechanism
clues.description: Beneath the séance table, a system of wires and levers connects to the gas lamps and a concealed bellows. Someone has gone to considerable trouble to manufacture supernatural effects.

clues.title: Vesper's Private Journal
clues.description: A leather-bound journal in Madame Vesper's hand. Most entries are theatrical notes — cues for cold reading, audience management. But the final pages shift tone entirely: frantic sketches of sigils, a repeated phrase in bastardised Latin, and the words 'It answered. God help me, it answered.'

clues.title: Pemberton's Gambling Debts
clues.description: Lord Pemberton owes eleven thousand pounds to a Limehouse syndicate. Lady Ashworth's late husband held the markers. If the séance 'proves' the spirit world is real, Ashworth's spiritualist foundation inherits the estate — and Pemberton's debts are called in.

clues.title: The Grey Dawn Sigil
clues.description: Carved into the underside of the séance table — invisible unless you know to look. A binding circle from the Hermetic Order of the Grey Dawn, designed to anchor a summoned presence to a physical location. It is not decorative. It is functional.

clues.title: The Dismissed Servant
clues.description: A footman named Harker was dismissed from Lady Ashworth's service three days ago for theft. He was seen loitering near the house this evening. Suspicious — but Harker was in a Bow Street holding cell at the time of the séance.

clues.title: Gerald Ashworth's Will
clues.description: A copy of the late Gerald Ashworth's last will and testament, found in Lady Ashworth's study. The key clause: the entire estate passes to the Ashworth Foundation for Spiritual Research if 'credible evidence of communication with the deceased' is produced within two years of Gerald's death. The deadline is in six weeks.

clues.title: Ritual Components Receipt
clues.description: A receipt from a Limehouse apothecary for 'consecrated salts, binding chalk, and essence of nightshade.' The buyer's name is illegible, but the delivery address is Lady Ashworth's townhouse. The receipt bears the faint impression of a brass stamp — the Court of Smoke serpent.

clues.title: The Court's Supplier
clues.description: A Court of Smoke intermediary — a thin man called Needle — supplied the ritual components to the Ashworth household. He dealt with a woman matching Vesper's description, but the payment came from a different source: a bank draft drawn on Lord Pemberton's account. Pemberton funded the real ritual while building the fake mechanism. He wanted both outcomes covered.

clues.title: Elara Thorne's Letters
clues.description: A bundle of letters from the vanished medium Elara Thorne to Gerald Ashworth, found hidden in a false panel in the séance room. The letters describe increasingly disturbing contact with an entity Elara calls 'the Presence.' The final letter, dated three days before Elara's disappearance, reads: 'It knows my name now. It knows where I sleep. Gerald, I am afraid. The binding is not holding.'

clues.title: Dr Whitmore's Account
clues.description: Dr Whitmore, a Rationalists Circle member among the guests, provides a clinical account of the séance. He noted the precise moment the manufactured effects ceased and the genuine manifestation began — a 'discontinuity' he cannot explain. He also observed that Pemberton's hands were not on the table during the levitation, contradicting Pemberton's claim of continuous contact.

clues.title: Gerald's Portrait
clues.description: A portrait of Gerald Ashworth in the hallway appears to have been recently moved — the wallpaper behind it is unfaded. Suspicious, but investigation reveals Lady Ashworth simply had it reframed last week. The portrait conceals nothing but grief.

clues.title: The Servant's Key
clues.description: A key found in the servants' quarters that opens the false panel in the séance room. The key is on a chain with a small Grey Dawn pendant — suggesting that at least one member of the household staff has connections to the Order.
